skill.name: gate-exchange-futures
skill.description: Guides Gate USDT perpetual futures workflows for opening, closing, canceling, and amending orders through Gate MCP tools.
skill.summary: Manage Gate USDT perpetual futures orders with confirmation-focused MCP workflows.
skill.author: gate

security_audit.summary: The static shell-execution, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by Markdown code spans, examples, and Gate MCP API names. No prompt injection or credential handling was found, but the skill intentionally enables high-impact Gate futures trading actions and needs marketplace warnings about real financial execution.

medium_findings.title: Real Futures Trading Actions Through Gate MCP
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to call Gate MCP tools that can open positions, close positions, cancel orders, and amend orders. These are legitimate stated capabilities, but they can create financial loss if triggered incorrectly or without clear user authorization.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced instructions explicitly call create, close, cancel, and amend workflows through Gate MCP. The behavior is intentional rather than malicious, but the impact is financially material.

medium_findings.title: Inconsistent Confirmation Coverage In Scenarios
medium_findings.description: Top-level rules require confirmations for major operations, but several scenario steps show direct order placement or cancellation without an explicit confirmation step. This increases the chance that an agent follows a scenario example and performs a high-impact action too quickly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files contain both confirmation requirements and scenario steps that omit confirmation. This is a clear documentation inconsistency, though the top-level safety rules partially mitigate it.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The many static Ruby backtick execution findings point to Markdown code spans, tables, and fenced examples. No evidence found that these files execute local shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are rendered Markdown examples or inline file and API names. They are not Ruby source code and contain no executable shell context.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm detections appear to match version text, headings, identifiers, or order examples rather than implemented cryptographic code. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, or signature routines in the reviewed locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe version formatting, documentation tables, descriptions, or example order IDs. They do not implement cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Matches Are API Query False Positives
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections are references to exchange account, order, position, or error-query workflows. No evidence found of host enumeration, file-system probing, or local system discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content concerns Gate exchange API validation and order error handling. It does not instruct local system reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Order Creation Tool Calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill repeatedly instructs use of create_futures_order for market, limit, reduce-only, and reversal workflows. This is not malware, but it is a high-impact external financial operation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact MCP tool name appears in order placement and reversal instructions. The risk is operational financial impact, not command execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bulk Order Cancellation Tool Calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes cancel_all_futures_orders workflows that can remove multiple open orders. This can change account risk exposure if performed without careful confirmation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced scenarios directly instruct bulk cancellation through Gate MCP. The behavior is legitimate but financially sensitive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Order Amendment Tool Calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs amendment of live orders by price and size. This can materially change execution risk for existing futures orders.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The tool call is clearly documented and confirmation is usually required. The remaining risk is the sensitivity of changing live order parameters.

content.value_statement: Manual futures order workflows can be error-prone and slow. This skill guides Gate MCP actions with structured checks, confirmations, and result summaries.
actual_capabilities: Routes open, close, cancel, and amend requests to focused futures workflows.
Uses Gate MCP tool names for contract checks, account checks, positions, and orders.
Converts USDT notional or base amount into contract size using contract metadata.
Handles single and dual position modes with different position and leverage APIs.
Prompts for confirmation before many high-impact order actions.
Summarizes order, position, cancellation, amendment, and exchange error results.
limitations: Requires a configured Gate MCP connection and account permissions.
Does not provide market monitoring, arbitrage scanning, or trading advice.
Cannot guarantee fills, prices, liquidity, liquidation outcomes, or exchange availability.
Some reference scenarios omit explicit confirmation despite the top-level safety rule.

use_cases.title: Prepare Futures Orders
use_cases.description: Convert user intent into a checked order plan with contract, side, size, price, margin mode, and leverage details.

use_cases.title: Reduce Or Reverse Positions
use_cases.description: Review current positions, close all or part of a position, or reverse direction with verification steps.

use_cases.title: Maintain Open Orders
use_cases.description: List, cancel, or amend existing futures orders while preserving order IDs and status details.

prompt_templates.title: Open A Simple Position
prompt_templates.prompt: Open a long BTC_USDT futures position for 1 contract at a limit price of 65000. Show the final summary before placing it.

prompt_templates.title: Close A Position
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my BTC_USDT futures position and prepare to close half of it. Show the size and expected action before execution.

prompt_templates.title: Cancel Selected Orders
prompt_templates.prompt: List my open BTC_USDT futures orders and help me cancel the selected orders after I choose them.

prompt_templates.title: Manage Dual Mode Leverage
prompt_templates.prompt: For ETH_USDT in dual position mode, prepare an isolated 10x market short order and use the correct position and leverage APIs.

output_examples.output: The assistant checks the contract, verifies margin and leverage context, presents a final order summary, and waits for confirmation before placing the order.
The assistant identifies open BTC_USDT orders, asks for confirmation when canceling all, submits cancellation through Gate MCP, and reports the results.
The assistant reviews the current order, compares the old and new price, asks for confirmation, amends the order, and verifies the updated status.

best_practices: Confirm every order placement, position close, reversal, batch cancellation, and amendment before execution.
Check contract metadata, account mode, position mode, and leverage before submitting orders.
Use read-only queries first when the user gives incomplete contract, side, size, or order details.
anti_patterns: Do not treat this skill as financial advice or an automated trading strategy.
Do not place market orders or reverse positions from ambiguous user intent.
Do not bypass confirmation because a scenario example shows a direct tool call.

faq.question: Does this skill handle real trading actions?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide Gate MCP calls that open, close, cancel, or amend futures orders.

faq.question: Does it store API keys or credentials?
faq.answer: No evidence found of credential storage in the reviewed skill files.

faq.question: Does it support spot trading?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed instructions focus on Gate USDT perpetual futures workflows.

faq.question: Can it calculate contract size from USDT notional?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the assistant to use mark price and quanto multiplier from contract metadata.

faq.question: Does every workflow require confirmation?
faq.answer: The top-level rules require confirmations for major actions, but some scenario examples omit explicit confirmation.

faq.question: Can it monitor markets or find arbitrage?
faq.answer: No. The README states that market monitoring and arbitrage scanning are out of scope.
